_meta.notes: The metropolis. Built where the five rivers meet at the estuary; every race has a quarter that originally housed only their own people, but most are mixed now. The Wash itself moves cargo north-south; the Spoke Roads radiate outward to the five homelands.

tagline: Five rivers, five peoples, one tide.
ambientPool: The bells of three quarters strike at slightly different intervals. The metropolis has never agreed on time.
A barge-master leans on a pole longer than themselves and corrects course with a single tap.
Salt and woodsmoke and inkdust mix in the air at the river's mouth.
Rain comes in from the east, off Saltgale, smelling of mudflat.
An orcish reader passes silently with three folios under their arm; an elven clerk steps aside without breaking conversation.

rooms.summary: The plaza at the city's heart. Five roads radiate outward to the five homelands. A bronze compass-rose set into the cobbles names them by direction; the inscription is in five scripts.
ambientPool: A messenger from Threadwarden stops at the SE marker, drops a bundle, runs back the way they came.
Three dwarven Whitebark cider-jars are stacked at the N marker, waiting for a cart that has not arrived.
Two halfling salt-keepers debate a price in low fast voices at the SW marker.
An elven Vellum sits on the bronze compass-rose itself, pretending to read.
A lone Hush-reader from the W spoke turns a page in a folio, then writes one careful word in the margin.

exits.narrative: South toward the riverside cargo quays.
East toward the residential quarters where each race built its first streets.
North into the elven-built arcade of counting houses and shipping desks.
West toward the orc-built paper row.

rooms.summary: Where the five rivers meet. Barges from each spoke unload here. The cargo language is a five-way pidgin nobody admits to speaking.
ambientPool: A barge from the Furrow line arrives heavy with Furrowmouth wheat.
Reef-cured fish are unloaded in identical waxed barrels, all stamped with the same anchor.
A Topgrove orchardist haggles in the wind-paced cadence with a Saltgale Salt-folk wholesaler over a cider shipment.
The tide comes in early today; ropes are retied without comment.

exits.narrative: Back uphill into the heart of the city.
East along the river to the residential quarters.

rooms.summary: Nominally five neighborhoods — Threadwarden, Saltgale, Highfield, Anchorage, Indices — but in practice every street has all five flags and at least one mixed family.
ambientPool: Children of three races chase each other through a side alley, shouting in pidgin.
An orcish window has a halfling drying-line of fish strung outside it.
A coldhammer's anvil rings once, then a Topgrove flute answers from two streets over.
The smell of dawn-meal halfling stew comes from the wrong window for it to be a halfling household.

rooms.summary: A long arched arcade lined with elven counting houses. Slate boards rewritten hourly with grain prices, salt prices, freight rates. The act is at full performance here.
ambientPool: An elven Branchman in unspoiled grey raises a single eyebrow at a slate that has just changed.
A Vellum smiles in a way that means a contract has just become enforceable.
Two Clearseers meet, do not quite acknowledge each other, and turn opposite directions.
The act drops for half a second when a Salt-folk wholesaler bursts in laughing — then comes right back up.

exits.narrative: South back to the central square.

rooms.summary: Orcish stationers, binders, and one Hush-reader's sanctuary. The street is the quietest in the city. Conversations are conducted in writing.
ambientPool: Three readers sit on a bench, each turning a page in a different rhythm.
A Marginal scribbles into the margin of a folio they have just bought; the bookseller waits.
A Cataloguer sorts a small stack of slips into invisible order and is satisfied.
The wind doesn't seem to make as much noise here. People notice and don't comment.

exits.narrative: East back to the central square.
